vocab.meaning: khả năng phân tích tinh thông
vocab.example: Detailed reading helps me sharpen my analytical prowess, allowing me to dissect every argument in a text.

vocab.meaning: xác định ý chính/nội dung cốt lõi
vocab.example: I typically use scanning to ascertain the gist of a news article before deciding if it's worth a closer look.

vocab.meaning: áp lực/tải trọng nhận thức lên não bộ
vocab.example: Scanning is my go-to when I want to reduce the cognitive load after a long, exhausting day at the office.

vocab.meaning: xem xét kỹ lưỡng các điều khoản/chi tiết nhỏ
vocab.example: When it comes to legal contracts, scanning is far too risky; you absolutely must scrutinize the fine print.

vocab.meaning: tình trạng quá tải thông tin
vocab.example: In this era of information overload, scanning is an essential survival skill to filter out irrelevant data.

vocab.meaning: sự chú ý tỉ mỉ đến từng chi tiết
vocab.example: Academic research demands meticulous attention to detail, which only deep, intensive reading can provide.

vocab.meaning: các điểm nổi bật/quan trọng nhất
vocab.example: If I am in a rush, I just scan the executive summary to pick out the salient points of the proposal.
For literary fiction, I always opt for detailed reading to ensure a truly immersive experience in the story's world.

vocab.meaning: việc đọc lướt sơ bộ ban đầu
vocab.example: I often do a preliminary skim of a textbook chapter to get my bearings before starting a deep study session.

vocab.meaning: phân tích văn bản (về cấu trúc, ngôn từ)
vocab.example: Scanning is useless for textual analysis, where you need to evaluate the author's choice of metaphors and tone.
